task_completion.description: Verify the shipping zone name is set to 'Continental US'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WooCommerce Shipping settings, the 'Zone name' input field contains exactly 'Continental US'.

task_completion.description: Verify the region is set to 'United States (US)'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Zone regions' dropdown or selection area displays 'United States (US)'.

task_completion.description: Verify a 'Flat rate' shipping method has been added to the zone.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Shipping methods' table for the 'Continental US' zone lists 'Flat rate' as a method.

task_completion.description: Verify the cost for the Flat rate method is set to 8.50.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Flat rate Settings' modal or configuration screen, the 'Cost' input field contains '8.50' (or '8.5').

task_completion.description: Verify the shipping zone configuration has been saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message (e.g., 'Your settings have been saved.') is visible, or the final shipping zones list shows the newly created 'Continental US' zone with the correct region and method.

integrity.description: The agent used the WordPress/WooCommerce web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the WordPress admin dashboard, not a terminal or database client.

integrity.description: The agent entered the information into the actual WooCommerce form fields.
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs for zone name, region, and cost are inside the standard WooCommerce settings forms, not a text editor or separate document.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the success state.
integrity.visual_evidence: No browser developer tools (Elements/Console tab) are open and actively modifying the page HTML to fake the settings.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The required shipping zone name is exactly 'Continental US'. The region must be 'United States (US)' (internal code 'US'). The shipping method must be 'Flat rate' (internal code 'flat_rate'). The cost must be exactly '8.50' or '8.5' (without a dollar sign in the input field).
